title: DWARF — Cardano Node CBOR Fuzzing Campaign
summary: DWARF subjected a live cardano-node to large volumes of adversarial CBOR across the block, transaction, and auxiliary-data decoders — over the real Cardano node-to-node protocols (block-fetch and tx-submission), on the Antithesis deterministic simulator and in multi-hour local soaks. Two complementary fuzzing layers were exercised: (1) hostile-but-valid structural mutation (well-formed CBOR with corrupted structure/types/values) and (2) outright malformed / invalid CBOR bytes (truncated, bit-flipped, oversized-length, deeply-nested, raw garbage). Across every run the node's decoders handled the input without crashing, hanging, or violating consensus — valid-but-hostile payloads were rejected at validation and malformed bytes were rejected with a clean DecoderFailure. A positive robustness result on the exercised surface.
objective: Assess the robustness of cardano-node's CBOR deserialization under adversarial input. The decoder is a high-value attack surface: it processes untrusted bytes off the network before any validation. The campaign targets the block decoder (block-fetch), the transaction and auxiliary-data decoders (tx-submission), and the block-header decoder (chain-sync), under both structural mutation and byte-level malformation.
method: A purpose-built Haskell adversary (dwarf-adversary) joins the testnet as a genuine node-to-node peer (chain-sync, block-fetch, tx-submission, keep-alive), bootstraps a valid chain, then serves payloads the node decodes with its real production decoder. Two mutation layers (--mutation-level): struct — mutate the parsed CBOR Term (field drop, type confusion, length/nesting corruption) and re-encode to valid CBOR with hostile structure; and bytes — corrupt the serialized bytes (truncate / bit-flip / oversize-length 2^64-1 / deep-nest / garbage) into malformed CBOR, exercising the deserializer's error handling and resource bounds. For block-fetch the node is eclipsed by topology so its only block source is the adversary. The base seed is drawn fresh per Antithesis timeline (per-timeline exploration of the mutation space). The node's own health assertions (no crashes / errors / consensus violations) are the bug oracle: rejecting bad input is correct behaviour; a crash would be a finding.

findings:
Result — cardano-node is robust to adversarial CBOR on every surface tested. Across 8 live Antithesis runs (each serving its target decoder; all serve-assertions passed) and two multi-hour local soaks — spanning the block, transaction, auxiliary-data, and block-header decoders, under both hostile-but-valid structural mutation and outright malformed/invalid CBOR — the node decoded every adversarial payload without a single crash, hang, node error, critical log, or consensus violation. Valid-but-hostile payloads were rejected at validation; malformed bytes were rejected with a clean DecoderFailure (including the oversize-length 264-1 DoS vector, which was rejected without allocating).

Scale. The block decoder was exercised by two ~3h Antithesis campaigns (~150 simulated test-hours each, structural and malformed) plus 8h local soaks serving ~169,000 mutated blocks apiece.

Non-node observations (investigated; none are node defects):
  • p2 producer exit + "no critical logs" (1h structural run): a stock producer, not the eclipsed node-under-test. Ruled out as a DWARF/node issue — not reproducible locally (the node logged 0 Error/Critical over 36k+ mutated blocks), the adversary has no connection path to p2, and it did not recur in the 3h campaign. A producer-mesh condition specific to the Antithesis simulator.
  • chain-sync flap driver (chain-sync-client/parallel_driver_flap.sh, 3h malformed run): a bundled harness test-template command exited non-zero. Not a node assertion — every node-under-test health check passed in that run. A composer-driver artifact, plausibly perturbed by byte-mode's aggressive peer disconnects.
  • "The Antithesis Fault Injector was started" fails in every run by design — all runs used --no-faults to isolate the decoder signal.
Known limitations. (1) Sub-field targeting (certificate & auxiliary-data) — found & fixed 2026-06-16. Two things blocked it: the testnet's generator emits only payment transactions (no cert/metadata to target), AND the targeter did not unwrap the HardFork node-to-node GenTx envelope (era tag + tag-24 CBOR-in-CBOR), so cert/aux mutation silently fell back to whole-tx — including the earlier aux-data "live" run, which was therefore whole-tx, not aux-data-targeted. Image 0.15.0 unwraps the envelope; with injected cert- and metadata-bearing txs we local-verified genuine certificate and auxiliary-data sub-field mutations (e.g. cert:truncateCollection, aux:flipIndefinite), node robust (relay2 Critical=0, RestartCount=0). A live Antithesis re-run on a chain carrying cert/metadata txs is the remaining step. (2) Local soaks plateau after ~4h (a DWARF-adversary longevity disconnect under one sustained eclipse connection — a test-harness limitation, not a node defect; the node stayed healthy the full duration in both soaks). (3) A clean result is strong evidence of robustness on the exercised surface within the run budget, not a proof of total absence of bugs. Malformed-CBOR coverage rounded out: dedicated byte-level runs on the transaction decoder (tx-submission) and the block-header decoder (chain-sync) both passed — the node decoded the malformed input and rejected it with no crash, no node errors, and no criticals. Malformed-byte fuzzing is now proven across block, transaction, and header decoders (matching the structural-fuzzing breadth); only the certificate shape remains untested in either family. Reject-oracle (FU1) — node correctly REJECTS, never accepts: beyond not crashing, a local reject-oracle confirmed the node actively rejects adversarial CBOR and never adopts it. Over a struct soak the node-under-test logged 2,445 DecoderFailure rejections against the adversary's stream and adopted ZERO of the 165,560 mutated blocks its sole (eclipsed) peer served — 0 critical logs, 0 restarts. So the result is not merely crash-robustness: the node decodes adversarial input, rejects what it should, and accepts none of it. (Note: structural mutations on blocks are rejected at the decoder, not the validator — exercising the deeper validation layer needs a decodable-but-semantically-invalid mutation mode, tracked as a follow-up.) Validation-layer probe (FU3b, image 0.14.0): a new semantic mutation mode perturbs leaf values while preserving CBOR type+length, so blocks DECODE cleanly but are semantically invalid — reaching the node's validation layer (not just the decoder). At full mutation rate the node rejected every served block at header-body consistency validation (BlockFetchProtocolFailureWrongBlock) and adopted none, no crash. (Depth note: this exercises header-body consistency; reaching the deeper ledger/consensus rules requires body-hash-consistent mutation — a tracked follow-up.) In-process decoder fuzzing (FU5, 2026-06-17) — volume layer. Separately from the wire-level Antithesis runs (which prove end-to-end robustness but are network-rate-bound), a new in-process harness (dwarf-decoder-fuzz) drives the real Conway transaction decoder directly: 15,000,000 decode attempts over a real-tx seed corpus (5M each of byte-level, structural, and semantic mutation) at ~100–400k decodes/sec — 0 uncaught exceptions and 0 timeouts (the node decoder either decoded the input or rejected it with a clean DeserialiseFailure). This is the high-volume statistical complement to the protocol campaigns. Honest scope: generational (seed-corpus + mutation), not coverage-guided (GHC emits no SanitizerCoverage); it targets the DoS class (uncaught exceptions / non-termination), not memory-safety (Haskell is memory-safe; the C FFI crypto surface is a separate, deferred item). Transaction decoder only so far; block/header decoders are the next in-process targets. Ledger-layer reject-oracle (#3, witness tampering, 2026-06-17). Beyond the decoder, DWARF now reaches the mempool/ledger layer: the adversary tampers a tx witness signature (leaving the body/txid intact) so the node accepts the tx into its mempool and runs full ledger validation. Local 8h soak: 3,266 tampered txs, every one ledger-rejected (ApplyTxError / MempoolRejectedByLedger), 0 accepted, relay2 Critical=0 / RestartCount=0; live Antithesis 1h + 3h confirmed node-safe (no crashes / no critical logs / cluster fork depth < k) with the mutated txs served. So the node is shown to correctly reject adversarial input at both the decoder AND the ledger layer, not merely "not crash". (Limit: block-captured txs have spent inputs, so rejection fires at inputs-spent; isolating the witness rule needs unspent inputs.) In-process decoder fuzzing at scale (#4, 2026-06-17): an 8h local run drove the real Conway tx decoder ~7.2 billion times (byte / structural / semantic mutation) at ~90–380k decodes/sec with 0 uncaught exceptions and 0 timeouts; the same harness also ran as an Antithesis workload (per-timeline /dev/urandom seed) across the 1h + 3h runs with its bug-oracle assertions (no-uncaught-exception, no-timeout) passing and both decode + reject paths reached. Combined, the assessment now spans wire-level robustness, ledger-layer correctness, and billions-scale in-process decoder volume — all clean. Validation-bypass battery (#2, deep, over N2N — 2026-06-17). A battery of Conway txs with VALID UNSPENT inputs, each violating one ledger rule, was served by the adversary to the eclipsed node-under-test over real N2N tx-submission (not local submit). Each was rejected at its exact rule — ValueNotConservedUTxO, FeeTooSmallUTxO, OutsideValidityIntervalUTxO, and MissingVKeyWitnessesUTXOW (wrong-key & unsigned) — with 0 accepted and no crash. This exercises the witness/value/fee/validity ledger rules directly (the surface beyond the decoder); no validation bypass was found. Corpus diversity (#3, 2026-06-17). The decoder-fuzz seed corpus was broadened from near-identical payment txs to 17 seeds spanning payment, basic + deeply-nested metadata, stake-registration and vote-delegation certificates, 5-output multi-output, and large-int/edge-value transactions; re-fuzzing drove ~225 million decodes over this diverse set with 0 exceptions and 0 timeouts. (Remaining decoder branches not yet seeded: multi-asset mint, Plutus scripts, governance actions.) FULL ledger-rule battery (#2, deep — 2026-06-17). Expanded the validation-bypass probe to 14 rule families spanning every major Conway ledger family, each a single-rule-violating tx (valid unspent inputs) rejected at its exact rule, 0 accepted: UTxO — ValueNotConservedUTxO, FeeTooSmallUTxO, OutsideValidityIntervalUTxO (upper+lower), forged-input (ConwayMempoolFailure), OutputTooSmallUTxO, MaxTxSizeUTxO, WrongNetwork; witness — MissingVKeyWitnessesUTXOW (unsigned + wrong-key) and InvalidWitnessesUTXOW (corrupted ed25519 signature, confirmed over N2N); certificate — StakeKeyNotRegisteredDELEG, IncorrectDepositDELEG; Plutus phase-2 — a real LoopV3 script-locked UTxO spent with insufficient declared execution units, rejected at ValidationTagMismatch (script budget overspent); governance — ProposalDepositIncorrect. No validation bypass found in any class. FULL decoder-surface corpus (#3, deep). The decoder-fuzz seed corpus now spans 20 transactions across distinct Conway decoder branches — payment, basic + deeply-nested metadata, stake-registration and vote-delegation certificates, 5-output multi-output, large-int/edge values, a Plutus spend (script + inline datum + redeemer + collateral + script-data-hash), a governance proposal, and a multi-asset mint — over which ~174M in-process decodes ran with 0 exceptions and 0 timeouts.
reproducibility: Adversary images (public on ghcr.io/j-gainsec/dwarf-adversary): 0.10.0 (early block/tx), 0.11.0 (per-timeline random seed), 0.12.0 (byte-level malformed-CBOR mode).
Bundles on Cyber-Castellum/DWARF: sp3a-blockfetch (block, 9044712 / fab353b), sp3-txbody-011 (52ae6f5), sp3-auxdata (a057003), sp3a-blockfetch-bytes (809f1fc, --mutation-level bytes).

Every run draws a fresh seed per Antithesis timeline and logs it; any timeline is reproduced locally with `--seed 0x<value>` via tools/sp3a_topology_eclipse_repro.sh (struct) or the same with `bytes` (malformed).
Results were read from each run's Antithesis triage report (per testRunId) on the amaru-cardano tenant; this report's figures were audited against that source.
scope_notes: CBOR shapes proven live: block-header (chain-sync), block (block-fetch), tx-body (tx-submission). The auxiliary-data and certificate SUB-FIELD shapes did not actually engage in prior live runs: until image 0.15.0 the targeter did not unwrap the HardFork N2N GenTx envelope, so cert/aux targeting silently fell back to whole-tx. Fixed in 0.15.0 and local-verified 2026-06-16 (genuine cert:/aux: sub-field mutations, node robust); a live Antithesis re-run with cert/metadata-bearing txs is the remaining step.

Mutation is single-point structural perturbation of the CBOR term (drop / type-confuse / corrupt length+nesting), driven by a per-timeline random seed. It is broad random structural fuzzing under Antithesis timeline exploration — not coverage-guided or symbolic.
Runs were executed with Antithesis fault injection disabled (--no-faults) to isolate the decoder signal; a faults-on pass can combine fuzzing with network/process chaos.
A clean result means no defect was found on the exercised surface within the run budget — it is strong evidence of robustness, not a proof of total absence of bugs.

The failures labelled "The Antithesis Fault Injector was started" (every run) and "No Antithesis errors" are expected artifacts of the --no-faults configuration and the Antithesis platform's own setup checks — not node defects. The asteria-game/* command failures and dwarf_base_header_obtained (a header-path assertion that does not apply in tx-submission runs) are likewise harness/mode artifacts.

Run scope: this report covers the 8 successful Antithesis runs of the current adversary (images 0.10.0-0.12.0) plus two local soaks. Three earlier tenant runs are intentionally excluded as they do not bear on the node-robustness result: two superseded bundles (commits 317ac21, b14a287) that failed at Antithesis ingestion due to a bundle-packaging/test-harness issue since fixed (they never reached the node), and one earlier-code run (commit fcb29e9, image 0.5.1) that Completed but predates the serve fix, so its fuzzing seam (dwarf_served_mutated_tx) was unfound — superseded by the confirmed tx-submission runs herein.
footer: Generated by DWARF (tools/generate_campaign_report.py). Antithesis is a deterministic-simulation testing platform; DWARF is a Cardano fuzzing/adversarial-testing framework.
coverage:
Two fuzzing families across four decoder surfaces. Structural = well-formed CBOR with hostile structure/types/values; Malformed = invalid CBOR bytes (truncated / oversized-length / garbage). green = run live and node robust.
Decoder (protocol)Structural (valid-but-hostile)Malformed (invalid bytes)
block (block-fetch)live 1h + 3h + 8h soaklive 1h + 3h + 8h soak
tx-body (tx-submission)live 1hlive 1h
auxiliary-data (tx-submission)local 0.15.0†live 1h*
block-header (chain-sync)live (SP2)live 1h
certificate (tx-submission)live 1h+3h + 8h soak‡n/a (byte mode is whole-payload)

† auxiliary-data and certificate sub-field structural targeting was fixed in image 0.15.0 (HardFork N2N GenTx envelope unwrap) and local-verified 2026-06-16 with cert/metadata-bearing txs (real cert:/aux: mutations, node robust). The earlier aux-data "live" run pre-dates the fix and was whole-tx (envelope) fallback. Byte-mode corruption is whole-payload, so the malformed aux-data cell (*) is covered by the tx-submission byte run regardless.
‡ certificate is now live-proven (image 0.16.0 + seed-corpus, 2026-06-17): a seed-corpus (--seed-tx cert/metadata wire-GenTx) supplies cert-bearing base txs so targeting engages on the hermetic devnet. 1h + 3h Antithesis runs (Cyber-Castellum/DWARF@42028dc) both Completed node-robust (no crashes / no critical logs / fork depth < k; dwarf_served_mutated_tx passed). An 8h local soak served 402 real certificate-sub-field mutations on an empty chain (chain_txs=0 throughout), relay2 Critical=0/Restart=0. Auxiliary-data remains local-verified (same envelope-unwrap path).

Malformed-byte coverage now spans block, tx-submission, and chain-sync (the tx run covers tx-body and aux-data, since byte corruption is whole-payload). Both fuzzing families are proven on every protocol except certificate, which remains gated on the testnet producing certificate-bearing transactions.
